chapter_title.en: Chapter 36 – The obedience and the piety

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (54/ 8)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (54/ 8)
text: يَا مَعْشَرَ الشِّيعَةِ شِيعَةِ آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ كُونُوا النُّمْرُقَةَ الْوُسْطَى يَرْجِعُ إِلَيْكُمُ الْغَالِي وَ يَلْحَقُ بِكُمُ التَّالِي فَقَالَ لَهُ رَجُلٌ مِنَ الْأَنْصَارِ يُقَالُ لَهُ سَعْدٌ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ مَا الْغَالِي قَالَ قَوْمٌ يَقُولُونَ فِينَا مَا لَا نَقُولُهُ فِي أَنْفُسِنَا فَلَيْسَ أُولَئِكَ مِنَّا وَ لَسْنَا مِنْهُمْ قَالَ فَمَا التَّالِي قَالَ الْمُرْتَادُ يُرِيدُ الْخَيْرَ يُبَلِّغُهُ الْخَيْرَ يُؤْجَرُ عَلَيْهِ

ثُمَّ أَقْبَلَ عَلَيْنَا فَقَالَ وَ اللَّهِ مَا مَعَنَا مِنَ اللَّهِ بَرَاءَةٌ وَ لَا بَيْنَنَا وَ بَيْنَ اللَّهِ قَرَابَةٌ وَ لَا لَنَا عَلَى اللَّهِ حُجَّةٌ وَ لَا نَتَقَرَّبُ إِلَى اللَّهِ إِلَّا بِالطَّاعَةِ فَمَنْ كَانَ مِنْكُمْ مُطِيعاً لِلَّهِ تَنْفَعُهُ وَلَايَتُنَا وَ مَنْ كَانَ مِنْكُمْ عَاصِياً لِلَّهِ لَمْ تَنْفَعْهُ وَلَايَتُنَا وَيْحَكُمْ لَا تَغْتَرُّوا وَيْحَكُمْ لَا تَغْتَرُّوا .

en.hubeali: Humeyd Bin Ziyad, from Al Hassan Bin Muhammad Bin Sama’at, from one of his companions, from Aban, from Amro Bin Khalid,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘O group of Shia, the Shia of the Progeny<sup>asws</sup> of Muhammad<sup>saww</sup>! Take a central position. The exaggerator would return to you and the ‘Ta’aly’ would catch up with you’. So a man from the Helpers called Sa’ad, said, ‘May I be sacrificed for you<sup>asws</sup>! What is the exaggerator?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘People who are saying regarding us<sup>asws</sup> (that which) we<sup>asws</sup> are not saying regarding ourselves<sup>asws</sup>. So those are not from us<sup>asws</sup> and we<sup>asws</sup> are not from them’. He said, ‘So what is the ‘Ta’aly’?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The apostate indenting the goodness. The goodness reaches him and he is Recompensed upon it’.

Then he<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> turned to face us and he<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> said: ‘By Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup>! There is no freedom (from the Fire) with us<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> from Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup>, nor is there between us<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> and Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> a relationship, nor is there an argument for us<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> against Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup>, nor do we<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> get closer to Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> except by obedience. So the one from you who was obedient to Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup>, our<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> Wilayah would benefit him, and the one from you who was disobedient to Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup>, our<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> Wilayah would not benefit him. Woe be unto you! Do not be decieved. Woe be unto you! Do not be deceived’.
en.sarwar: 6. Hamid ibn Ziyad has narrated from al-Hassan ibn Muhammad ibn Sama’a from certain individuals of his people from Aban from ‘Amr ibn Khalid from abu Ja’far (a.s.) who has said the following: “Abu Ja’far (a.s.) has said, ‘O the community of Shi’a, Shi’a of Muhammad (a.s.) be the central support so that both al- Ghali and al-Tali refer to you.’ A man from Ansar (people of Madina) called Sa’d, said, ‘May Allah keep my soul in service for your cause, what is the meaning of al-GhaliV The Imam said, ‘They are the people who say about us what we do not say about ourselves. Thus, they are not of our people and we are not of their people.’ The man then asked, ‘Who are al-TaliV The Imam said, ‘They are those who search for good. They receive good instructions (from our Shi’a) and are rewarded for it.’ The Imam then turned to us and said, ‘I swear by Allah that we do not possess any amnesty from Allah and there is no family relation between Allah and us nor do we possess any authority over Allah. We do not seek nearness to Allah through any other means but obedience to Him. Whoever of you is obedient to Allah it (our guardianship, Wilayah ) will benefit him, and whoever of you is disobedient to Allah our guardianship, Wilayah, will not benefit him. What is the matter with you? Do not be misled! What is the matter with you? Do not be misled!”’
